meta.name: DAR-5 Dimensions of Anger Reactions
meta.description: The DAR-5 is a 5-item ultra-brief scale that rapidly assesses anger problems across five dimensions: frequency, intensity, duration, aggression, and interference.
meta.author: Forbes et al. (2014)
meta.reference: Forbes D, et al. A new measure of anger: The DAR-5. Journal of Clinical Psychology. 2014;70(8):781-789.
meta.instruction: Over the last 4 weeks, to what extent have you experienced the following?

questions.text: I often find myself getting angry at people or situations
When I get angry, I get really mad
When I get angry, I stay angry
When I get angry at someone, I want to hit or clobber the person
My anger prevents me from getting along with people as well as I'd like to

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your anger level is within the normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good emotional management skills
Consider learning stress management techniques as prevention
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Mild
ranges.description: You may occasionally experience excessive anger. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Learn to recognize early warning signs of anger
Practice deep breathing and relaxation techniques
Try the 'pause' strategy — give yourself a few seconds to cool down before reacting
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

ranges.label: Moderate
ranges.description: Your anger is notable and may be affecting your relationships and daily life. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider learning anger management strategies
Cognitive reappraisal — re-examine the thoughts triggering your anger
Regular exercise can help reduce anger
If anger problems persist, consider consulting a professional
Consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: Severe
ranges.description: Your anger problems are significant and may substantially affect your relationships, work, and quality of life. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional psychological help
Anger management group therapy or individual therapy can be very effective
Learn nonviolent communication skills
If you have violent tendencies, please seek help immediately
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
